user_prompt: Can you draw a coordinate grid and plot two points — let's call them A at (1, 2) and B at (7, 6)? Then draw a right triangle by adding a third point C at (7, 2) so that AC is horizontal and BC is vertical. Label all three points, show the right angle at C, and mark the lengths of the two legs. I need to see how the hypotenuse AB connects the original two points.
